channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Chiwenga stuns Zanu-PF @bulawayo24 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Vice-President Constantino Chiwenga reportedly urged senior Zanu-PF officials not to celebrate the passage of the controversial Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3), reminding party members that... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Zimbabwe’s political stagnation @bulawayo24
item.description: The passage of Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3) has reignited debate over Zimbabwe’s political future, with analysts and opposition figures arguing that the proposed constitutional changes c... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Zupco wins reprieve as Bulawayo halts depot repossession @bulawayo24
item.description: Public transport operator Zimbabwe United Passenger Company (Zupco) has successfully appealed against the repossession of its Bulawayo depots after convincing the local authority that it is making mea... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: ZACC busts lithium smuggling syndicate @bulawayo24
item.description: A joint operation by the Zimbabwe Anti-Corruption Commission (ZACC) and the Zimbabwe Revenue Authority (ZIMRA) has uncovered an alleged lithium smuggling syndicate involving local suspects and a forei... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Amacimbi thief arrested In Beitbridge @bulawayo24 @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: A 40-year-old man has been arrested in connection with a violent robbery in Beitbridge in which cash and five large bags of Mopani worms were allegedly stolen.The Zimbabwe Republic Police identified t... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Political campaign behind Chinhoyi farm dispute @bulawayo24
item.description: A long-running dispute over Dundrennam Farm in Chinhoyi has intensified, with the Bosman family alleging they are the targets of politically motivated attempts to force them off their land through occ... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Zimbabwe’s US$2.3bn procurement system flagged as high corruption risk @bulawayo24
item.description: Public procurement has emerged as one of Zimbabwe’s biggest fiscal vulnerabilities after a new assessment found that contracts worth more than US$2.3 billion are being processed through a system weake... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Tear gas terror in sex workers crackdown @bulawayo24
item.description: A late-night police operation targeting suspected sex workers in Hurungwe has drawn criticism after residents alleged that tear gas deployed during the raid affected innocent families, including pregn... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Zimbabwe man trapped in mercenary plot @bulawayo24
item.description: A Hwange man has appeared before the Harare Magistrates’ Court on human trafficking charges after allegedly recruiting five Zimbabweans to travel to Russia under the false promise of employment, befor... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: ARTUZ petitions Human Rights Commission over teacher’s death @bulawayo24
item.description: The Amalgamated Rural Teachers Union of Zimbabwe (ARTUZ) has lodged a formal complaint with the Zimbabwe Human Rights Commission (ZHRC), accusing the Public Service Commission of Zimbabwe (PSC) of sys... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Polygamy Gone Wrong: Woman Allegedly Burns Co-Wife To Death As Husband Escapes @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: Woman Allegedly Sets Bedroom Ablaze With Co-Wife And Husband Inside A polygamous relationship has ended in tragedy after a woman allegedly set fire to a building where her co-wife and husband were sleeping, killing the younger woman. The husband reportedly escaped the flames but later collapsed and lost consciousness. The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) said [...] The post Polygamy Gone Wrong: Woman Allegedly Burns Co-Wife To Death As Husband Escapes appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Veteran Journalist Mkhululi Sibanda Dies
item.description: By A Correspondent | Zimbabwe’s journalism fraternity is mourning the death of veteran journalist and editor Mkhululi Sibanda, who passed away yesterday... Continue reading "Veteran Journalist Mkhululi Sibanda Dies" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Abatwa achirombesa vanhu nenyoka: 19-year-old sangoma arrested after being caught with wealth-giving cobra and puff adder snakes
item.description: Mutare – A 19-year-old self-proclaimed traditional healer, Gift Kanodaweta, also known as James Malunga, has been apprehended in Mutare for allegedly operating without proper documentation and possessing two live, venomous snakes. The arrest, which occurred last weekend in the Chikanga area, has brought to light concerns about fraudulent traditional healing practices and the illegal trade [...] The post Abatwa achirombesa vanhu nenyoka: 19-year-old sangoma arrested after being caught with wealth-giving cobra and puff adder snakes first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Bogus employment agent in court over alleged Russia jobs scam that landed Zimbabweans on warzone and left one victim dead
item.description: A HWANGE man accused of luring Zimbabweans with promises of lucrative jobs in Russia, only for them to allegedly end up fighting in the Ukraine war, has appeared in court facing human trafficking charges after one of the alleged victims reportedly died. Oscar Sifelani Mtshiya (48), of Empumalanga Extension Township in Hwange, appeared before Harare [...] The post Bogus employment agent in court over alleged Russia jobs scam that landed Zimbabweans on warzone and left one victim dead appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.description: On 1 July, an Airbus A330 lifts off from Harare for London under the (via The Mutapa Times) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
